commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer handling for the protected command-execution policy change plus contributor real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: The diff narrows command-execution allowlist behavior and adds targeted tests; I found no new dependency, workflow, secret, permission, or supply-chain exposure. (sha=faa66b680da2f585265d9a21549682b04ceb0661)
commands.review_summary:
This PR threads allowlist context into node-host `system.run` execution planning, rechecks rebuilt POSIX safe-bin shell argv before dispatch, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. By source inspection, current main rebuilds POSIX safe-bin shell argv after allowlist success and dispatches it without a second allowlist evaluation; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer handling for the protected command-execution policy change plus contributor real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: Recheck rebuilt system.run argv [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: rewritten system.run shell argv could be returned after the initial allowlist decision without checking the final rendered payload.
- Solution: re-run allowlist evaluation on rebuilt shell argv before it is used for execution.
- What changed: carried allowlist context into execution planning and added focused regression coverage for accepted and rejected rebuilt argv.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no config defaults, command syntax, or public API behavior changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

- system.run should execute the same command structure that passed allowlist policy. Rebuilt argv now has to satisfy the allowlist before execution proceeds.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: rebuilt system.run shell argv is gated by a final allowlist evaluation before execution.
- Real environment tested: local Linux checkout using the repository Node test wrapper.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts -t "rewrites safe-bin shell payloads|rewrites nested safe-bin shell chains"`
- Evidence after fix: both commands completed with passing test files; the new regression file reported 2 passing tests, and the targeted existing system-run file reported 2 passing tests with unrelated tests skipped by filter.
- Observed result after fix: rebuilt argv is returned only when the final allowlist check passes; otherwise execution planning returns a mismatch.
- What was not tested: full suite, live external integrations, and remote execution lanes.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): N/A

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: shell argv rebuilding happened after the first allowlist decision and reused the prior segment metadata as the trigger for rendering.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no second allowlist check against the rebuilt shell payload.
- Contributing context (if known): safe-bin and inline-chain rendering intentionally transforms shell payloads before execution.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: rebuilt shell argv is denied when the final rendered payload does not satisfy allowlist inputs, and accepted when it does.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the exact helper that rebuilds argv without depending on the full invoke pipeline.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing system-run tests cover safe-bin rewrite success paths.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[allowlist decision] -> [rewrite argv] -> [execute]

After:
[allowlist decision] -> [rewrite argv] -> [final allowlist check] -> [execute or deny]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: command execution planning now fails closed when rebuilt argv does not pass allowlist evaluation.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node test wrapper in local checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): default test fixtures only

### Steps

1. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts`.
2. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts -t "rewrites safe-bin shell payloads|rewrites nested safe-bin shell chains"`.

### Expected

- Rebuilt argv fails closed when the final allowlist check fails and still executes in existing valid rewrite scenarios.

### Actual

- Both focused test commands passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: direct rebuilt-argv pass/fail regression tests and existing system-run safe-bin rewrite paths.
- Edge cases checked: final allowlist miss after rebuild, final allowlist pass after rebuild, nested safe-bin shell chain rewrite path.
- What you did **not** verify: full suite, live external integrations, and remote execution lanes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: valid rewritten commands could be rejected if the second pass does not receive the same allowlist context.
  - Mitigation: the execution phase now carries the same approvals, safe-bin policy, trusted dirs, skill-bin state, cwd, and env into the final check; existing rewrite success tests were also run.
target.author: pgondhi987
